ideal_use_cases: Semiconductor fab tool installation
Petrochemical vessel and column erection
Wind turbine nacelle and blade installation
Bridge girder placement
Nuclear power plant heavy lift
LNG plant module installation
Stadium structural steel erection
Shipyard keel laying and block placement
notes_for_planner: Crawler cranes require significant site preparation including crane mat installation, ground bearing capacity engineering, and large laydown area for boom assembly. Lead times for large capacity units (600T+) can be 4-8 weeks. Best suited for lifts requiring full pick-and-carry travel or sustained stationary heavy lifts. Confirm boom configuration (standard, luffing, superlift) based on radius and capacity requirements.

ideal_use_cases: High-rise residential and commercial construction
Hospital and healthcare tower builds
Data center structural and equipment installation
Concrete core slipform construction
Curtain wall panel placement at height
Long-duration campus construction
Multi-tower simultaneous construction
notes_for_planner: Tower cranes are the right choice when project duration exceeds 3 months and continuous overhead lifting is required throughout construction. Luffing jib variants are required in urban areas with adjacent structures within the standard horizontal jib swing radius. Foundation design must account for mast base loads. Climbing collars allow jump-climbing with concrete pours. Most effective when paired with a rigger and signalperson on each floor level.

ideal_use_cases: Transformer and switchgear installation at substations
Steel erection for low-to-mid-rise structures
HVAC rooftop unit placement
Industrial equipment installation and replacement
Prefabricated module placement
Bridge beam setting
Emergency crane mobilization
notes_for_planner: Mobile cranes offer the best combination of capacity and mobility for projects requiring rapid setup and repositioning. Road permits are required for travel on public highways for units over 80,000 lbs GVW. Outrigger extension areas must be confirmed prior to deployment. Hydraulic telescoping boom allows capacity tradeoff for radius — always confirm chart at the required working radius, not maximum capacity.

ideal_use_cases: Indoor equipment setting in factories and cleanrooms
Glass curtain wall installation on high-rise facades
Rooftop equipment replacement via roof hatch access
Casino and hospitality interior chandelier and signage
Elevator and escalator equipment installation
Utility room equipment replacement in occupied buildings
Stone and marble slab placement in luxury interiors
Mechanical room access in hospitals
notes_for_planner: Spider cranes (UNIC, Maeda, Jekko) are the only solution when access is restricted to elevator shafts, personnel doors, or roof hatches. Maximum width in transport configuration is typically 28-36 inches. Weight in transport configuration ranges from 1,500 to 8,000 lbs depending on model — verify floor loading capacity before deployment. All-electric models are required for cleanroom and food-grade environments. Battery runtime limits consecutive shift length.

ideal_use_cases: Construction site material and steel placement on soft or uneven ground
Rural utility and pipeline work
Agricultural and remote infrastructure projects
Military base construction
Precast concrete element placement
Oil field equipment installation
notes_for_planner: Rough terrain cranes are purpose-built for sites where all-terrain or hydraulic truck cranes cannot safely operate due to ground conditions. Four-wheel drive and oscillating axles provide traction and stability without outrigger pads in some conditions. Not licensed for travel on public roads at speed — requires flatbed transport between distant sites. Consider rough terrain crane when site access road is unimproved or when crane must work within a muddy active construction site.

ideal_use_cases: Utility substation transformer and switchgear setting
Industrial boiler and chiller installation
Rooftop HVAC unit replacement on commercial buildings
Precast bridge girder setting
Wind turbine component erection at accessible sites
Plant and refinery maintenance turnarounds
Tilt-up construction panel bracing
Modular skid placement
notes_for_planner: All-terrain cranes are the most versatile option in the crane fleet — multi-axle all-wheel-drive allows self-propelled highway travel and offroad positioning. Most commonly used for single-day or short-duration lifts where mobilization speed and repositioning flexibility matter. Confirm outrigger pad bearing area requirements — many urban jobs require specialized outrigger pads or cribbing due to underground utilities or underground parking. Required permits vary by jurisdiction for boom travel on roads.

ideal_use_cases: On-site material distribution on active construction sites
Lumber and drywall placement on multi-story buildings
Masonry and block material placement
MEP prefab rack placement at elevated locations
Agricultural building erection
Rental yard material handling
Modular home component placement
notes_for_planner: Telehandlers (also called reach forklifts or telescoping boom forklifts) are not classified as cranes but frequently appear in the same procurement as crane equipment. Their key advantage is simultaneous fork function and telescoping reach, eliminating a separate forklift on smaller sites. Models with jib attachments can function as light-duty cranes. Check stabilizer extension requirements and tire load rating for upper-floor ramp access.

ideal_use_cases: MEP rough-in work at height in warehouses and industrial facilities
Facade and window cleaning access
Lighting installation in arenas and convention centers
Overhead crane installation in industrial buildings
Bridge inspection
Wind turbine nacelle access for maintenance
Telecom antenna installation on towers
notes_for_planner: Aerial lifts (boom lifts, scissor lifts, articulating lifts) are work positioning equipment, not load-lifting cranes. They are often specified alongside crane work for worker access at elevation. Articulating boom lifts are preferred when working around obstructions. Scissor lifts require near-level surfaces. Outdoor-rated units are required for wind exposure above 28 mph. Electric models are mandatory for sensitive indoor environments.

ideal_use_cases: Industrial plant maintenance lifts in confined plant corridors
Tilt-up concrete panel erection on warehouse sites
Shipyard maintenance and ship repair lifts
Pick-and-carry in oil refineries and petrochemical plants
Port terminal cargo handling
Paper mill and steel mill maintenance
Pump, valve, and heat exchanger replacement in plants
notes_for_planner: Carry deck cranes are designed for confined plant environments where no other crane configuration can maneuver. Their 360-degree rotation and low profile allow operation under pipe racks, conveyors, and structural steel. Load capacity drops significantly with outriggers retracted — always verify pick-and-carry chart at the required travel path. Available in diesel and electric (explosion-proof) variants. Explosion-proof models are required in Class I Division 2 refinery and chemical plant hazardous areas.

ideal_use_cases: Steel erection for low-rise commercial buildings
Telecom tower antenna and equipment installation
Sign and billboard installation
Utility pole and power line work
Light commercial HVAC installation
Pre-engineered metal building erection
Lumber yard and material supply delivery and placement
notes_for_planner: Boom trucks provide the fastest mobilization in the crane fleet — they drive to the job site and set up in minutes. Best for lifts under 5 tons at moderate radii where a full mobile crane would be cost-prohibitive. Articulating knuckle boom models offer reach around obstacles that telescopic booms cannot achieve. Check local bridge and road weight limit restrictions on planned travel routes. Not suitable for lifts requiring certified rigging plans or engineered lift plans.

ideal_use_cases: High-rise construction in dense urban cores where jib overswing into adjacent airspace is prohibited
Airport proximity construction with FAA height restrictions
Multi-tower sites where crane jibs must avoid collision
Condominium and hotel towers in tight city blocks
notes_for_planner: Luffing jib variants trade capacity at radius for the ability to raise the jib vertically, dramatically reducing the footprint of horizontal swing. Required when city permitting restricts crane swing over adjacent properties or public right-of-way. Erection cost and complexity is higher than fixed-jib tower cranes. Anti-collision systems are typically required when two or more luffing jibs share airspace on the same project.

ideal_use_cases: Low-rise residential construction (2-6 stories)
School and institutional construction
Renovation projects requiring crane access without full mast
Rapid-deployment jobsites with short durations
Precast residential community construction
notes_for_planner: Self-erecting tower cranes fold down for transport and erect in 1-2 hours without a mobile crane assist, dramatically reducing setup cost. Capacity and hook height are limited compared to conventional tower cranes. Well-suited to high-volume residential developers building repetitive low-rise product. Not appropriate for concrete high-rise or heavy steel structures.

ideal_use_cases: Shipyard hull block and module placement
Nuclear power plant component handling
Steel mill and foundry coil and ingot handling
Wind turbine nacelle and blade storage and transfer
Rail maintenance yard wheel and axle replacement
Paper mill roll handling
notes_for_planner: Gantry cranes are permanent or semi-permanent fixed installations, not mobile crane equipment. They appear in the CraneGenius context primarily during new facility construction (when an owner needs a temporary gantry to set in a permanent one) or during plant expansions where temporary gantry rental supports construction activities. Engineered rail and runway systems require structural analysis prior to loading.

ideal_use_cases: Bridge superstructure sliding and launching
Offshore platform topsides load-out
Power plant turbine and generator installation
Stadium roof and truss installation
Industrial press and heavy machinery relocation
Modular refinery unit skid placement
notes_for_planner: Hydraulic strand jack and hydraulic gantry systems are the preferred solution when the load exceeds what a conventional crane can lift within the available radius, or when the lift geometry requires synchronized multi-point lifting. These systems are specialty rigging solutions provided by a limited number of heavy lift specialists nationally. Typical lead time for planning and mobilization is 4-12 weeks. Requires full engineered lift plan.

ideal_use_cases: Moving heavy loads across a site without re-rigging
Precast concrete element delivery and placement at remote ends of large sites
Paper mill and steel mill equipment repositioning
Large industrial facility module placement
Mine site haul truck and shovel component handling
notes_for_planner: Pick and carry cranes must have their travel path engineered — ground bearing pressure under rubber tires with a suspended load can exceed 200 psi. Dedicated crane mats or reinforced haul roads are required for most applications. Travel speed is limited when load is suspended. Consider all-terrain crane for infrequent repositioning; only specify pick-and-carry when frequent multi-point travel with load is required.

ideal_use_cases: Offshore platform installation and maintenance
Underwater pipeline and cable lay support
Harbor structure and pier construction
Salvage operations
Ship-to-shore container handling
Jacket and topsides float-over operations
Wind farm monopile installation offshore
notes_for_planner: Marine cranes aboard derrick barges and heavy-lift vessels are a specialty segment outside the scope of most land-based crane companies. However, onshore marine terminal construction regularly requires land-based cranes working at the water's edge, often with special outrigger engineering due to proximity to waterway or wharf edge. Coordinate with marine contractor for barge crane complement when onshore crane radius is insufficient for over-water picks.

ideal_use_cases: Large format glass panel installation on curtain wall systems
Stone slab and tile placement in luxury interiors
Precast concrete facade panel repositioning
Solar panel installation on rooftops
Sheet metal and aluminum plate handling in fabrication
Windshield and automotive glass placement in assembly
notes_for_planner: Vacuum lift systems are below-the-hook attachments, not standalone cranes. They require a host crane (typically a spider crane or all-terrain crane) for elevation and positioning. Battery backup safety requirement is mandatory for all vacuum lifters used in overhead lifts to prevent dropped load in case of power failure. Cup design must match the surface — textured, porous, or coated glass surfaces may require specialized cups. Wind speed limits apply to outdoor facade work.

ideal_use_cases: Stadium roof and large truss lifting
Nuclear reactor head and component replacement
Bridge deck launching and sliding
Offshore topside installation
Industrial press installation
Transformer and large electrical equipment jacking and skidding
notes_for_planner: Strand jacks use high-strength steel strands threaded through a hydraulic jack to lift or lower loads with extreme precision. Multiple jacks are synchronized electronically to maintain load balance within millimeters. This is a highly engineered, specialty lift requiring a dedicated lift engineering team. Used when load exceeds crane capacity, geometry prevents crane access, or precision placement tolerance is extremely tight. Very long lead time for planning.

ideal_use_cases: MEP installation and inspection at height in warehouses
Arena and convention center overhead rigging
Bridge inspection and maintenance access
Window cleaning and building maintenance
Telecom antenna installation in right-of-way
Signage installation on commercial storefronts
Painting and coating of elevated structures
notes_for_planner: Articulating boom lifts (also called knuckle booms) provide the most versatile access around obstacles at height. Straight telescoping boom lifts provide maximum horizontal outreach. Scissor lifts are preferred for vertical-only work on flat surfaces. Outdoor lifts are wind-rated to ANSI/SAIA A92 standards — ensure proper model selection for outdoor exposure. Operator certification (OSHA 29 CFR 1926.453) required for all operators.

typical_capacity_range: varies by component — 1 ton to 1000+ tons
ideal_use_cases: Tandem and tailing lifts for long or tall loads
Below-the-hook custom lifting frames for semiconductor tools
Spreader beam assembly for wide structural sections
Equalizer beam systems for multi-point lifts
Skidding and rolling systems for horizontal load movement
Chain hoist and come-along systems for positioning
Personnel fall arrest integration during elevated rigging
notes_for_planner: Rigging systems are the engineered interface between the crane hook and the load. All below-the-hook lifting devices must comply with ASME B30.20 and require proof load testing and annual certification. Custom lifting frames for semiconductor or precision equipment require manufacturer-approved rigging plans. Rigging accessories including shackles, wire rope slings, chain slings, and synthetic round slings must be inspected before each use and replaced per ASME/OSHA criteria. Always specify the rigging accessories in the lift plan — omitting rigging from the cost estimate is a common source of bid errors.

ideal_use_cases: Horizontal movement of transformers in confined substations
Semiconductor tool relocation within cleanroom without ceiling clearance for crane
Industrial press and machine tool relocation on factory floors
Modular building and data hall placement
Bridge girder launching and sliding
notes_for_planner: Skidding systems (Hilman rollers, air casters, hydraulic skidding) move loads horizontally without vertical lift and are the solution when overhead clearance is insufficient for crane access. Air casters float loads on a thin film of compressed air and require smooth, sealed concrete floors. Hilman rollers work on uneven surfaces but require push force. Hydraulic skidding systems are computer-controlled for precision positioning. These systems are often procured alongside crane services for complex tool install projects in semiconductor fabs.

ideal_use_cases: Self-propelled modular transporter (SPMT) module moves at refineries
Power transformer road transport from rail yard to substation
Wind turbine tower section and blade transport to site
Industrial press and heavy machinery cross-country transport
Bridge beam and girder transport from fabrication yard to site
notes_for_planner: Heavy transport (oversized load permitting, pilot car requirements, route surveys) is a prerequisite for many of the lift scenarios in this library. Crane companies frequently coordinate with heavy transport specialists as part of a single lift package. Key planning items: state and county oversize/overweight permits, bridge load ratings on transport route, utility line clearances, and nighttime travel windows required by many states for loads over 16 feet wide.
